preamble: The correspondence between the voiced alveolar fricative [z] and the lateral [l] is primarily documented through rhotacism-like shifts and liquid correspondences, where voiced sibilants transition through a rhotic stage before lateralizing in specific dialects.

examples.note: Documentation of complex sibilant-liquid alternations where [z] corresponds to lateral sounds in specific regional varieties.
Sporadic lateralization of historical rhotics (some derived from sibilants via rhotacism) in localized speech patterns.

sources: Phonological research on Turkic and Romance liquid-sibilant correspondences.
